sessions.intent: Define failing-test contract for moving per-action LLM teaching from C# attributes/Compile.llm to markdown files at os/system/modules/<module>/.

before.assumptions: Architect plan in .bot/compile-llm-notes-per-action/architect/plan.md is approved. Catalog entry (app.goals.goal.steps.step.actions.action.@this) already exposes Description/ModuleDescription/Examples — coder will add Notes/ModuleNotes (action-level + module-level), populate Description/ModuleDescription/Examples from markdown after migration script runs, and surface text fields per the merge-at-render rule. Provider→Code attribute rename already in source.
before.risk: Drift cases are LLM-sensitive; assertions must accept the structural shape (no spurious channel/Message) without overfitting to literal prompt text. Loader/renderer tests reference a markdown-fixture filesystem; need to fit existing MockFileSystem patterns rather than touching real os/system/modules/. Modifier folder rename (AiModule/ → ai/) is part of branch but I am NOT testing rename — only the loader behavior on the new lowercase paths.

actions.context: Drift cases — the load-bearing pair from the architect's brief. Plang `--test` goals; bodies are `throw "not implemented"`. Commentary pins the pre-fix shape that is forbidden and the architect's 3-fresh-cache-builds rule.

Mechanism tests for the markdown teaching loader: per-layer reads (module vs action), merge order (module first, blank line, action), and orphan-file warnings. New folder `CatalogTests/` per the project alias-clash convention (PLang.Tests/GlobalUsings.cs aliases `Data`). TUnit `[Test]` + `Assert.Fail("Not implemented")`.

Renderer tests: per-action blocks only render for planner's set; modifier actions render via same path; system-prompt size on a Tests/Simple step compile drops below 16 KB (architect verification check #1).
Provider→Code rename guard. Source already shows [Code] everywhere — guards against the old name reappearing and pins that PLNG001 diagnostic text teaches [Code].

after.health: All test files written failing-by-construction per character contract. Two plang drift tests + 22 C# tests across 5 files.
after.notes: User skipped per-batch approval. Coder is next; the two drift cases are the load-bearing tests — verify across 3 fresh-cache builds (CLAUDE.md "Stale-binary trap").

sessions.intent: Verify coder v1 against the test-designer's contract on a clean build, then mutation-test the load-bearing C# rules and attempt the architect's 3-fresh-cache drift validation.

before.assumptions: Coder v1 (commit 067c1e1b7) closed the drift cycle by migrating per-action teaching to markdown under os/system/modules/<module>/*.md and gating the renderer block on the planner's action set. The 22 C# tests were authored failing-by-construction; coder should have made them pass. The two plang drift cases (.test.goal) should also pass via 3 fresh-cache builds of Tests/Simple.
before.risk: Stale-binary trap when running plang --test; pre-existing wedges in the builder pipeline; drift tests passing-by-no-execution (Stale) if .pr files weren't built.

actions.context: Clean rebuild (rm -rf bin/obj for all four projects + dotnet build PlangConsole). 0 errors. Full C# suite via TUnit binary: 2942/2942 pass. 22 new tests across 5 classes all pass on first run.

Four mutations to verify the C# tests pin load-bearing behavior — all 4 caught, source reverted clean. (1) MergeLayers order swap caught by MergeTests; (2) ModuleStem orphan-skip removal caught by OrphanTests; (3) notes/modNotes constructor swap caught by LoaderTests; (4) planStep.actions gate removal in template caught by RenderTests (5/6 fail).

F1 (medium): .test.goal files committed without .pr companions. plang --test reports both as [Stale] — assertions never run. plang build wedges before reaching them on a pre-existing retry-path bug (Cannot convert Dictionary to Boolean) that fires earlier on this branch via Publish.goal (planner returned 10 vs 9 steps). Inner wedge pre-existing on parent; outer trigger is branch-shape-sensitive. Coder v2 must ship .pr files (built or seeded) so plang --test reports 2 pass.
Bot artifacts: plan, verdict (md + json), summary.

after.health: C# layer green and mutation-verified; committed Tests/Simple/.build/start.pr exhibits the correct post-fix shape (no channel=, no Message=). One load-bearing finding: drift .test.goal files Stale (.pr never built).
after.notes: Coder v2 next. Required fix: ship .pr files for the two drift cases. Optional: unwedge plang build pipeline so 3-fresh-cache rule is mechanically reproducible.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v2 (.test.goal reshape + DriftCaseArtifactTests) and v3 (real-builder .pr regeneration via --build={"files":[…]}) closes tester v1 F1.

before.assumptions: Coder v3 commit message claims 3-fresh-cache: 206 total, 206 pass, 0 stale, ~9s per round. Verify independently on a clean rebuild.
before.risk: Stale-binary trap (use clean rebuild). LLM variation across builds could shift non-load-bearing shape — check whether drift assertions still hold.

actions.context: Clean rebuild + C# suite 2945/2945 pass (+3 DriftCaseArtifactTests from v2). Architect's 3-fresh-cache rule reproduced independently with --build={"files":["Tests/Simple/Start.goal"],"cache":false}: round 1/2/3 = Pass/Pass, Pass/Pass, Pass/Pass; 0 Stale on any round. Mutation test (inject "name":"channel" into start.pr) correctly fails the drift case; reverted clean.

Non-blocking: fresh-build start.pr differs from coder v3's committed version (added Type=string parameter to two variable.set calls, flipped one Value type tstring→string). Drift assertions hold on both shapes — real LLM variation within the unconstrained-by-architect surface. Flagging only so a future reviewer doesn't mis-read the diff.
Bot artifacts. summary.md updated to v2.

after.health: Branch ready for next pipeline stage (security). F1 closed by coder v3. C# layer + plang drift layer + mutation tests all green.
